title: 10 best herbal teas for constipation
intro_desc: Herbal teas for constipation are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are known to promote bowel movements and provide relief from constipation. These teas typically contain natural laxatives, such as senna, licorice root, or ginger, which help to stimulate the digestive system and encourage regular bowel movements. By drinking herbal teas for constipation, individuals can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with irregular bowel movements, improve their overall digestive health, and enhance their quality of life. For example, someone who has been experiencing constipation due to a change in diet or lifestyle may find relief by drinking a soothing cup of chamomile and ginger tea before bed. Similarly, a person who has been struggling with chronic constipation due to a medical condition may benefit from consuming senna tea regularly, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Senna teas helps with constipation because of its natural laxative properties. The herb senna contains anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the muscles in the intestines to contract and move food through the digestive system more quickly. This increased muscle contractions help to soften and clear out stool, making it easier to pass and relieving symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort. Regular consumption of senna tea can also help regulate bowel movements and improve overall digestion.
remedy_properties: Sennosides: Senna tea contains sennosides, which are anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation by increasing intestinal water absorption and reducing gut motility.

Fiber content: Senna tea is a rich source of dietary fiber, which helps to regulate bowel movements, soften stool, and increase the frequency and consistency of bowel movements, making it easier to pass stools.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Senna tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of constipation such as bloating, abdominal pain, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sennosides, a natural laxative that helps stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Bark: Contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have been shown to increase stool frequency and soften stool texture.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried senna alexandrina leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured senna leaves with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for seven to ten minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Enjoy the warm, slightly bitter tea immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cape aloe teas helps with constipation because its unique combination of polysaccharides, saponins, and anthraquinones stimulates digestion, relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, and increases bowel movements. The mucilages present in cape aloe help to soften stool and make it easier to pass, while its natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce irritation and inflammation in the gut. This results in regular and comfortable bowel movements, alleviating symptoms of constipation.
remedy_properties: Aloin: Aloin, a type of anthraquinone glycoside, helps to stimulate bowel movements and increase stool frequency by softening stool and reducing colonic transit time.

Barbaloin: Barbaloin, another type of anthraquinone glycoside, has laxative properties that help to relieve constipation by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system and softening stool.

Fiber content: Cape Aloe tea contains a high amount of dietary fiber, which helps to regulate bowel movements, soften stool, and prevent constipation by adding bulk to stool and promoting regular bowel habits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which help to stimulate bowel movements and soften stool.
Bark: Contains a high concentration of aloin, a natural laxative that can help relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe ferox leaves at dawn or before sunrise for maximum potency retention.
Cut off the thick green skin from each leaf and slice it into thin pieces weighing approximately 20-30 grams.
Combine the sliced aloe pieces with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding any remaining pulp and solids.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days allowing it to chill before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alder buckthorn teas helps with constipation because it contains anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the muscles in the digestive tract to move stool through the intestines and increase bowel movement frequency. Additionally, the tea's ability to soften stools makes them easier to pass, reducing straining and discomfort associated with constipation. This natural remedy also helps restore normal bowel function by regulating bowel habits and relieving symptoms of chronic constipation.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements, increase stool frequency, and soften stool consistency, making it easier to pass a bowel movement.
Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in Alder Buckthorn tea help to reduce muscle spasms in the intestines, which can contribute to constipation, allowing for smoother passage of stool.
Fiber content: The tea is rich in soluble fiber, which helps to absorb water and increase stool bulk, making it easier to pass and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Bark: Contains tannins and flavonoids that help soften stool and increase its bulk, making it easier to pass.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried alder buckthorn bark per eight ounces of boiling water.
Add the measured bark to a heat-resistant cup or teapot and pour in the boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the tea's medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container if desired.
Enjoy the tea hot, sweetened with honey if preferred, within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turkish rhubarb teas helps with constipation because it is a natural laxative that stimulates bowel movements and relieves symptoms of irregularity. The herb contains anthraquinone glycosides, which work by increasing the frequency and softness of stool passage, reducing straining during defecation, and helping to prevent hard, dry stools from forming in the colon. By promoting regular bowel function, Turkish rhubarb teas can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort associated with constipation.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds stimulate the bowel muscles to contract, increasing stool motility and helping to alleviate constipation by promoting regular bowel movements.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation.
Fiber content: Turkish Rhubarb tea contains a significant amount of fiber, which helps to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass and reducing the risk of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation.
Bark: Contains alkaloids that have a gentle laxative effect, helping to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rhubarb roots and combine 2 tablespoons of the root with 4 cups of cold filtered water.
Heat the mixture in a medium saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to steep.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool before straining the tea into a large pot or teapot.
Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired, then serve hot or chilled as preferred.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow dock teas helps with constipation because of its natural ability to stimulate bowel movements. The herb's high concentration of anthraquinones, specifically aloin and aloe-emodin, works as a gentle laxative, increasing the frequency and ease of bowel movements. Additionally, yellow dock tea's mucilaginous properties help soften stool, making it easier to pass, while its anti-inflammatory effects may also reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders that can contribute to constipation.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: Yellow dock tea contains anthraquinones, which are known for their laxative effects by increasing stool frequency and softening stools, helping to alleviate constipation.

Tannins: Tannins in yellow dock tea have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to constipation, and also help to bind and remove toxins from the body.

Saponins: Saponins, another bioactive compound present in yellow dock tea, have been shown to stimulate the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is essential for maintaining a healthy gut microbiome and preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation by increasing the frequency and reducing the duration of stools.
Bark: Contains sennosides, a natural laxative that helps to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yellow dock leaves and stems from a trusted source or purchase dried yellow dock herb from a reputable vendor.
Rinse the yellow dock herb with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried yellow dock herb or 2 tablespoons of fresh yellow dock leaves with 8 oz of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with constipation because it stimulates digestive function, softens stool, and soothes irritated bowels. The mucilages in marshmallow root create a protective barrier that coats the intestinal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation, while its gentle laxative properties help move stool through the digestive system more efficiently. This natural remedy can provide relief from symptoms of constipation, including bloating, discomfort, and difficulty passing stool.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow root's demulcent property forms a soothing, protective film on the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, helping to relieve constipation by reducing irritation and inflammation.
Fiber content: Marshmallow root is a rich source of dietary fiber, which can help add bulk to stool, promoting regular bowel movements and relieving constipation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to chronic constipation by slowing down digestion and bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, making it effective in relieving constipation.
Bark: The bark contains high amounts of saponins, which have a gentle laxative effect, helping to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers fresh from the plant and rinse with clean water.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried or 2 tablespoons of fresh herb material with 8 oz boiling water in a tea infuser.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the mucilages to dissolve and the flavor to extract into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb solids from the liquid.
Enjoy hot or let cool to room temperature for a soothing and calming herbal infusion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow teas helps with constipation because it contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the digestive tract. This allows for smoother passage of stool and reduces inflammation in the colon, making bowel movements easier and more regular. Additionally, high mallow's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm irritability in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and reducing symptoms of constipation.
remedy_properties: Muclilage: The muclilage content in High Mallow tea helps to soften and lubricate stool, making it easier to pass through the digestive system and reducing symptoms of constipation.
Fiber: High Mallow tea contains a type of fiber called pectin, which can help increase the bulk of stool and promote regular bowel movements, thereby alleviating constipation.

Aperient properties: The aperient properties of High Mallow tea stimulate the digestive system, increasing intestinal contractions and helping to eliminate waste from the body more effectively, which can help alleviate symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the high mallow plant are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, easing constipation symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the high mallow plant contains saponins, which have a gentle laxative effect and help to stimulate bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried malva sylvestris leaves and flowers in a clean glass container measuring out 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to a rolling boil in a large pot then reduce heat to simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the measured malva sylvestris leaves and flowers to the pot stirring gently to combine with the hot water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes or until the tea reaches desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a teapot or individual cups using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth pressing on solids to release all liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Flax teas helps with constipation because of its natural laxative properties, which stimulate the digestive system to move food more efficiently through the colon. The mucilage in flaxseeds helps to soothe and soften stool, making it easier to pass. Additionally, flaxseed is high in fiber, which adds bulk to stool and helps to regulate bowel movements. As a result, drinking flax teas can help to relieve constipation symptoms such as bloating, discomfort, and irregularity.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Flaxseed contains a high amount of soluble fiber, which helps to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements, making it easier to pass stools and reducing symptoms of constipation.

Omega-3 fatty acids: The omega-3 fatty acids in flaxseed have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and also improve gut motility.
Lignans: Flaxseed is rich in lignans, a type of phytoestrogen that has been shown to stimulate bowel movements and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to relieve constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Flax roots are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, easing bowel movements.
Seeds: The oil-rich seeds of the flax plant contain omega-3 fatty acids that help regulate bowel function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried flaxseeds into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Add one cup of boiling water over the flaxseeds and let it steep for five to ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another cup, discarding the solids.
Combine the resulting tea with honey to taste, if desired, and serve warm or chilled.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with constipation because of its natural lubricating properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The plantain leaf, rich in mucilage, forms a protective barrier on the intestinal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. This allows for easier passage of stool and regular bowel movements, relieving symptoms of constipation such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. As a gentle and non-habit-forming remedy, herbal plantain teas can be enjoyed regularly to maintain healthy digestion and alleviate recurring constipation issues.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Plantain tea contains soluble fiber, which helps to soften stool, increase bowel movement frequency, and promote regularity by adding bulk to stool and preventing constipation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation and other digestive issues.

Antioxidant activity: Plantain tea's antioxidants, including vitamin C, vitamin E, and beta-carotene, help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and mucilage, plantain roots help soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, promoting regular bowel movements.
Bark: Containing astringent properties, plantain bark helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the colon, making it easier to pass stools.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantain leaves from your garden or store-bought package in sufficient quantity for desired brew strength.
Rinse the harvested plantain leaves with clean water to remove dirt and debris before proceeding with preparation.
Combine one teaspoon of dried plantain leaves or two teaspoons of fresh plantain leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a large tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the plantain leaf mixture in hot water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to extract and blend with the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or cup, discarding the solids and serving immediately while warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Psyllium husk teas helps with constipation because it contains a soluble fiber that absorbs water in the intestine, softening stool and promoting regular bowel movements. The fiber also increases the movement of food through the digestive system, allowing for more efficient elimination of waste products. Additionally, psyllium husk tea can help to increase the number of beneficial gut bacteria, which can further support healthy digestion and prevent constipation.
remedy_properties: Soluble Fiber: Psyllium husk is high in soluble fiber, which helps to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass and reducing the risk of constipation.
Prebiotic Properties: Psyllium husk acts as a prebiotic, feeding the good bacteria in the gut and promoting their growth, which can help regulate bowel movements and reduce symptoms of constipation.
Water Absorption: Psyllium husk is able to absorb water in the colon, increasing stool weight and making it softer and easier to pass, helping to relieve constipation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Psyllium seeds are used to make tea because they contain soluble fiber, which helps to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass.
Roots: Psyllium roots are used to make tea due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of psyllium husk powder into a clean cup or mug.
Add 8 ounces of boiling water to the cup and stir until the mixture forms a gel-like consistency.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the saponins to dissolve and the flavor to develop.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup to remove any remaining husk particles.
Drink the tea immediately, as the gel-like texture is most beneficial when consumed freshly prepared.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat constipation with herbal teas is by consuming varieties known to stimulate digestion and promote regularity. Senna, a natural laxative, is commonly found in tea blends and can help relieve constipation. Additionally, licorice root, fennel, and ginger teas can soothe the gastrointestinal tract, reduce inflammation, and encourage bowel movements. For optimal results, drink at least one to two cups of these teas daily, and ensure proper hydration by consuming plenty of water. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to constipation that are treated with herbal teas include bloating, cramping, and indigestion. Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile are known for their carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and bloating. Fennel tea is also commonly used to treat digestive issues as it contains antispasmodic properties that can help to soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, some herbal teas, such as dandelion or senna, have a mild laxative effect and can help to stimulate bowel movements, making them useful in treating constipation. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas for medicinal purposes, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition or are taking any medications.
